hypothesis: Accounting for process heating energy (from reactant heat capacities and phase-change enthalpies) and estimated purification costs (from predicted solubility/volatility) will render the Ca3SiO5+SiO2 synthesis route more cost-effective than CaO+SiO2 for Ca2SiO4 production whenever the synthesis temperature exceeds 1250 °C or the market price of CaO increases by more than 35%.
